page: Microsoft Azure E64d_v5 is an EDv5 family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 2458 GB of bundled local storage, but does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier results in multi-threaded stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is also robust, demonstrating high read and write bandwidth for large, uncached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 25% of tested systems, alongside high static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance metrics, such as single-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average range. Large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%.

From a resource-density perspective, the E64d_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads due to its high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and the inclusion of substantial local storage. This configuration reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput data operations. The server is suited for memory-optimized and database-centric workloads, including Redis deployments, relational database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is less optimal for workloads dependent on single-core speed or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E64d_v5 is an EDv5 family virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 512.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks, and top-tier database and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance and large LLM text generation remain average to weak. This memory-optimized configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications by bundling substantial local storage with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is suited for relational databases, Redis caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64d_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 Intel vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, built for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors and bundled local storage for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM
2458 GB of bundled local NVMe storage
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Average single-core and weak large-scale LLM inference speeds
Suited for relational databases, Redis, and software compilation
